---
title: "Module Title"
subtitle: "Bayesian Analysis Workshop"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
execute:
  warning: false
  message: false
---

## Learning Outcomes

By the end of this module, participants should be able to:

1. ...
2. ...
3. ...

## Research Scenario

State the applied research problem in social science or education language.

## Frequentist Framing

Show how participants would usually analyze or interpret the question.

## Bayesian Reframing

Translate the question into likelihood, prior, and posterior terms.

## Model

Specify the model in words, notation, and R code.

## Prior

Explain the prior choices and check their implications.

## Fit

Fit the model or inspect a precomputed fit.

## Interpret

Interpret posterior estimates, credible intervals, posterior probabilities, and practical thresholds.

## Check

Use posterior predictive checks and diagnostics.

## Report

Write a short methods or results paragraph.

## Practice

Complete the participant task.

## Takeaway

Summarize the main idea in two or three sentences.
